videos.text: 7/ Here's a demo our team recorded of Gemini 3.5 Flash driving a real KiCad task, via the @antigravity (agy) CLI and Cua Driver. https://t.co/s8bjtm72k7

1/ We had early access from @GoogleDeepMind to Gemini 3.5 Flash's native Computer Use. On Cua-Bench it posted the highest mean reward of any frontier model we tested - 0.267, on KiCad tasks no model fully solves. At Flash speed and cost. https://t.co/Hm01NEuOAv
6/ Headless agent over SSH builds a working budget in a real Linux spreadsheet on a remote box with no GPU and no display attached https://t.co/QbC8jKy6MN
5/ Wayland multi-window control, in preview. Cua Driver drives 16 cursors across 16 Wayland windows at the same time, each one moving independently. https://t.co/qdz70RyQbC
4/ Background dev loop. Claude Code builds a calculator app, drives the calculator UI, QAs its own work. The agent terminal stayed frontmost the whole time https://t.co/6wqF1ak1dL
3/ True multi synthetic pointer support. Cua Driver drives several cursors dragging the word "Cua" across XFCE at the same time. Main window stays frontmost. https://t.co/qVAwZNfd1W

1/ Today we're bringing Cua Driver to Linux: background computer-use for any agent. Hermes, Claude Code, Codex, or your own loop can drive real Linux desktop apps (X and Wayland in preview) through CLI or MCP while your desktop stays usable https://t.co/TyRNz4NBC9

1/ Today we're launching Cua-Bench with @SnorkelAI: a benchmark for computer-use agents on professional software, open for any model to run. The benchmark covers 25 expert-authored KiCad tasks, and the best frontier model we tested cleared only 6 of them. https://t.co/AM7EIotW6F

1/2 The Cua team is excited to join @aiDotEngineer World’s Fair on July 1 in SF to talk Computer-Use 2.0. CUAs are moving beyond screenshot + cursor loops toward window-scoped loops orchestrated by coding agents. We’ll share what changed and what becomes possible next. https://t.co/UCTBn8tfg9
^ EDITED https://t.co/14pm2qHOPF

Legacy Windows automation. We drove an old mail-service desktop app with no API. The kind of software that still runs real operations inside enterprises, governments, logistics, healthcare, and finance. For apps like this, the desktop window is the interface.

4/6 https://t.co/EM1iyJcZgM

Coding agent QA loop. Prompt: "build a beautiful WPF app and make sure it works."

Claude Code wrote the app, launched it, inspected the real Windows UI, found rough edges, fixed them, QA'd the result, then had Cua record the demo.

3/6 https://t.co/mQq2Te3pfA

Personal assistant. Agent fires off a Messages reply while I keep typing in my editor. The Space never switches.

7/8 https://t.co/6KzxE5bC5d

Pulling visual context out of apps I'm not looking at. Here's Claude Code playing a YouTube scene in a Chrome tab I never raised.

6/8 https://t.co/ZYQ9hcRSmU

Delegated demo capture. Agent drives the app, cua-driver record and renders zoom-on-click at export. Imagine just delegating an entire product demo to an agent, background computer-use now unlocks this.

5/8 https://t.co/PgHRDvl1fo

Background dev loop. Claude Code reproes a visual bug, writes the fix, QAs it. My agent harness stayed frontmost the whole time.

4/8 https://t.co/GUWu6ok7Rs

We've been using Cua-Bench internally—and with customers—for the last few months to evaluate every computer-use agent we deploy. 

Today it's open-source. 15 public tasks, 40 variations, adapters for OSWorld and Windows Agent Arena. One CLI, self-hostable. https://t.co/uKE6DziDgd

Today we're announcing cua-bench: a framework for benchmarking, training data, and RL environments for computer-use AI agents. Why? Current agents show 10x variance across minor UI changes. Here's how we're fixing it. https://t.co/nD0JTFHWjJ

1/3 We've just added Claude Opus 4.5 support to the Cua VLM Router and Playground - and you can already see it running inside Windows sandboxes. Early results are seriously impressive, even on tricky desktop workflows. https://t.co/bxRVOPeVw3

2/4 Deploy a sandbox, connect to Playground, and start sending prompts. Watch your agent interact with the desktop in real-time with full tool call visualization.

Multi-model support: Claude Sonnet 4.5 and Haiku 4.5 https://t.co/pDSurhsPnJ

2/4 Cloud Windows 11 sandboxes hot-start in under a second and persist everything between sessions. No more local Windows Sandbox resets or complex VM setups depending on Windows Sandbox or KVM.

3 sizes available:
• Small: 2 cores, 8GB RAM (8 credits/hour)
• Medium: 4 cores, https://t.co/SBzZz9RwHN

Here's Sonnet 4.5 completing the same task - 3 minutes vs 2 minutes.

The real insight: Haiku 4.5 (50.7% OSWorld) vs Sonnet 4.5 (61.4%) isn't just a small model - it's last quarter's frontier model at 1/3 the cost.

Benchmark full OSWorld for $200. Previously this cost $600 per https://t.co/CS9qGEYq8F

We tried Claude Haiku 4.5 on a computer-use task and it's faster + 3.5x cheaper than Sonnet 4.5:

"Create a landing page of Cua and open it in browser"

- Haiku 4.5: 2 minutes, $0.04
- Sonnet 4.5: 3 minutes, ~$0.14

This changes the economics of agentic automation ↴

1/2 https://t.co/nzvQIQ1JT7

Cua ❤️ open-weight models

Highly requested by the Discord community, we tested Moondream3 and Salesforce GTA-1 for UI grounding in computer-use agents

1/3 https://t.co/QwpMpTqo9d

We ran one of our hardest computer-use benchmarks on Anthropic Sonnet 4.5, side-by-side with Sonnet 4 ↴↴

1/4 https://t.co/zQ3pOkgd27

Day 5 of 5 Days of Cua: Introducing Human-in-the-Loop. Sometimes the best agent is you. Instant handoff from AI to human control when tasks need further judgment.

1/5 https://t.co/0qh6cphi9s

New mask. Same task. Cua is rebranding: from🖱️ pointer to 🐨 Cua-la, our new mascot.

1/3 https://t.co/dm8FNCiDRZ

You can now use the open-source GLM-4.5V model with the Cua Agent framework.

1/6 https://t.co/GzO8sHgqG6

GPT-5 for Computer-Use agents. Same tasks, same grounding model - we just swapped GPT-4o → GPT-5 as the thinking model. Left = 4o, right = 5. Watch GPT-5 pull away.

1/2 https://t.co/eXl8tJ2R2B

Bringing Computer-Use to the Web - control cloud desktops from JavaScript/TypeScript, right in the browser.

1/6 https://t.co/V7vuTCZheL

Introducing App-Use - create virtual desktops for AI agents to focus on specific apps.

1/6 https://t.co/ApHNu7yr5Y
c/ua ❤️ Docker. Introducing Lumier: run macOS VMs on Docker containers in minutes with minimal setup https://t.co/6V5FaZs7WT
We vibe-photoshopped a cofounder using C/ua https://t.co/jQ7jv4XV50
The era of local Computer-Use AI Agents is here. https://t.co/85TavU9ORR
C/ua now supports agent trajectory replay. Here's a behind-the-scenes look at it in action, thanks to one of our awesome users. https://t.co/40THdpnxic
Cua loves MCP. Launching official MCP Server support today - enabling our Computer-Use Agent to run through Claude Desktop, Cursor, and other MCP clients. https://t.co/ct4o5zHYri
Shoutout to our amazing contributor @iaktech for building tlume - a CLI tool that converts Tart VM images into the format expected by Lume! https://t.co/Igjkxv0szo
We've just started sending invites for the internal preview of Computer and Agent. If you're an early tester, check your inbox! We’d love your feedback. https://t.co/snGWI3o582

CUA Agent of Agents – In 2025, CUA agents will infuse AI into your apps and copilots. Example? A CUA agent using Browser + Cursor to set you on the right track to solving a GitHub issue https://t.co/Rf779tOYVJ

Our CLI just hit 1K stars in under 24 hours and even trended on @hackernews! Huge thanks to everyone who supported us in our first release. Stay tuned as we reveal more about our mission and what we've been cooking up over the past few months. Drop by the comments for a hint! https://t.co/H2uIe9MMs7

3/ What makes it work: each agent opens a named session tied to one window, and its cursor only touches that window, so they don't collide. Inputs run through both accessibility tree and vision, and an agent clicks and types in a background window without raising it. https://t.co/VO4B1J729l

2/ To celebrate #MSBuild tomorrow, here it is running on Windows: @NousResearch's Hermes subagents drive 4 windows of the same app at once, each cursor its own color, none touching your keyboard or moving the real mouse. https://t.co/kUQz5ocqQj

1/ This week our team at @trycua worked hard to ship cua-driver v0.5. The headline is a new primitive, Agent Cursors: many background agents can now each own a cursor, scoped to a non-overlapping action space. https://t.co/vAjRWd4t5e

Desktop and mobile control capabilities of Claude are great, but they're not new or exclusive to Anthropic's models. The community has been building interface agents since November last year. Check out a demo of a real iOS device (not an emulator!) being controlled by an AI agent https://t.co/P33XTfCI6n

What if resumes could speak? Would you prefer listening to a candidate's story instead of scrolling through a bunch of tech keywords? Here's mine fed to NotebookLM. Pretty impressive, I'd say! It's not every day that you find two podcasters vouching for your accomplishments! https://t.co/OSegDfG9Jc

Imagine if language models could tap into the app ecosystem of your iPhone. Would the need for plugins and assistants become obsolete if we simply allowed a model to orchestrate our existing (and many years robust) user interfaces?
